title: 10 best herbal creams for eye swelling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin, acemannan, and vitamin E that work together to reduce puffiness and alleviate discomfort. When applied topically around the eyes, these compounds help to calm inflammation, ease tension, and promote gentle hydration, resulting in a reduction of swelling and a more refreshed appearance. This natural remedy is particularly effective for alleviating dark circles, puffy bags under the eyes, and overall eye fatigue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye inflammation and damage, promoting healthy vision and reducing oxidative stress.

Moisturizing and soothing effects: Herbal aloe creams' moisturizing and soothing effects help calm irritated eyes and reduce swelling by hydrating the skin and eyelids, while also providing a protective barrier against environmental irritants.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most widely used part of the aloe plant, containing a high concentration of gel-like substance that helps to soothe and reduce inflammation in swollen eyes.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems, contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called aloin, which is effective in reducing eye swelling and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Grind one tablespoon of dried echinacea root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Add one cup of cooled boiled water to the ground echinacea root and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids after five minutes of pressing.
Mix two tablespoons of pure aloe vera gel with one tablespoon of coconut oil and three drops of tea tree essential oil in a small bowl for two minutes.
Combine the strained echinacea liquid with the aloe vera mixture and stir well until it forms a smooth, creamy consistency after five minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with eye swelling because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory properties and soothing astringents. The cream's active ingredients, including witch hazel extract and chamomile, work together to reduce puffiness and redness by constricting blood vessels and calming irritated skin. As the cream is absorbed into the delicate skin around the eyes, it helps to shrink swollen blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation, leaving the area looking smooth, calm, and refreshed. This natural remedy provides effective relief from eye swelling, making it an excellent option for those seeking a gentle yet potent solution.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Witch Hazel contains tannins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidants: The high antioxidant content in Witch Hazel helps protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to puffiness and swelling.
Astringent properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention, making it an effective remedy for reducing eye swelling and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, the roots help reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, making them effective in alleviating eye swelling.
Barks: With its high concentration of flavonoids and triterpenoids, the barks exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, soothing eye irritation and reducing puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and dry them using shade drying or air circulation for 2-3 weeks.
Combine 200g of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves with 500ml of boiling water in a stainless steel pot.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard solids.
Mix the cooled herbal infusion with 150g of beeswax, 50g of coconut oil, and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency, and pour into glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredient, calendula extract, contains flavonoids and triterpenoids that have a soothing effect on the skin and reduce redness and puffiness around the eyes. When applied topically, the cream helps to constrict blood vessels and alleviate fluid buildup, resulting in reduced swelling and a more radiant appearance. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the delicate eye area, leaving it feeling soft and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold creams contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which helps to reduce swelling and alleviate puffiness around the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams help to neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye swelling and inflammation, promoting a more even and healthy appearance.
Soften and soothe skin: Pot marigold contains beta-carotene, which helps to soften and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Flowers: Containing carotenoids and flavonoids, flowers have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that combat eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry calendula flowers in small quantities to ensure maximum potency within 3 hours.
Combine 200g of dried calendula flowers with 400ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks to allow for infusion and straining.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids after 2 hours.
Add 20g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil to the infused oil mixture and heat gently until melted within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with eye swelling because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce puffiness and discomfort. The herbal extracts in these creams, such as Echinacea purpurea, have been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin, including the delicate area around the eyes. By applying a Kansas coneflower cream directly to the affected area, you can quickly alleviate swelling, dark circles, and redness, leaving your eyes looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Kansas coneflower may help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye swelling by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antioxidant activity: The plant's antioxidant properties could help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to inflammation and swelling.

Immune-modulating effects: Some studies suggest that Kansas coneflower extracts may have immunomodulatory effects, potentially helping to reduce the body's inflammatory response and alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are effective in reducing swelling and redness associated with eye irritation.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and saponins, seeds help soothe and calm irritated eyes, alleviating discomfort from swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Combine 20g of echinacea angustifolia with 40g of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 hours allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids.
Whip 10g of beeswax with 20g of coconut cream and 20ml of infused echinacea oil until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with eye swelling because it contains berberine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces puffiness and redness. The cream's soothing properties calm irritated eyes, while its antibacterial properties combat infections that can cause inflammation. Additionally, goldenseal's vasodilatory effects help to reduce congestion and promote healthy blood flow, further reducing the appearance of swollen eyelids. This natural remedy provides effective relief from eye swelling and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and scavenging free radicals.

Berberidin: Berberidin, a flavonoid present in goldenseal, has been shown to have potent anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in reducing eye swelling caused by allergic reactions or inflammation.

Cinchona alkaloids: Cinchona alkaloids, including quinine and quinidine, have vasoconstrictive properties that can help reduce blood flow to the affected area, thereby decreasing edema (swelling) and promoting faster healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in alkaloids, particularly berberine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain a higher concentration of berberine than the roots, making them a popular choice for eye creams to treat swelling and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried goldenseal root and 10 grams of beeswax pellets for the base formula.
Mix 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil with 20 milliliters of witch hazel liquid in a double boiler setup.
Add the weighed goldenseal root to the oil mixture and simmer for 30 minutes, then strain the solution.
Combine the filtered oil extract with 10 grams of beeswax pellets in a small saucepan and heat until melted at 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the melted wax and oil mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and harden at room temperature within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with eye swelling because it contains a natural diuretic compound called taraxasterol, which effectively reduces puffiness and inflammation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness, providing fast and effective relief from swollen eyelids. Additionally, the cream's gentle and non-irritating formula makes it suitable for daily use, allowing you to enjoy a clear and bright visual experience with ease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, help reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness around the eyes by blocking pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including flavonoids and polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye swelling, promoting a healthier eye environment.

Vasodilatory: Dandelion's vasodilatory properties help improve blood circulation around the eyes, reducing fluid buildup and swelling by increasing the removal of excess fluids and waste products through lymphatic drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and redness associated with eye swelling.
Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing the appearance of swelling.
remedy_recipe: Grind 200g of dried taraxacum officinale root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered taraxacum officinale root with 100g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl until well combined for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat in a double boiler, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes or until the oil turns golden brown.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before adding 20g of beeswax and mixing until fully incorporated for 5 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into small glass jars and store in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The cream's active ingredients, such as allantoin and vitamin K, work together to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid retention around the eyes. Additionally, plantain has natural soothing and calming effects that help ease irritation and discomfort caused by eye swelling. As a result, using herbal plantain creams can provide quick relief from swollen eyes, leaving them feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Plantain has antioxidant properties that help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Soothing and cooling properties: The mucilages present in plantain can provide a soothing and cooling effect on the eyes, helping to reduce puffiness, redness, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce puffiness and ease discomfort associated with eye swelling.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the plantain plant contains starches that help soothe and calm irritated skin around the eyes, reducing inflammation and redness.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried leaves of plantago major and measure out 20 grams for the recipe.
Combine dried plantago major leaves with 200 milliliters of warm water in a glass bowl.
Steep mixture for 15 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth into another container.
Mix strained liquid with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which are able to penetrate deep into the skin and reduce puffiness. The cream's ability to constrict blood vessels also helps to diminish dark circles under the eyes. Additionally, stinging nettle has natural antioxidant properties that help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from environmental stressors and promote a more even tone. As a result, herbal stinging nettle creams are often used as a natural remedy for reducing eye swelling and promoting a brighter, more youthful appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salicylic acid and flavonoids, help reduce puffiness and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: Nettle cream's antioxidant agents, like polyphenols and carotenoids, combat oxidative stress and free radicals that can contribute to eye swelling, promoting overall eye health and reducing inflammation.

Vasodilatory properties: The vasodilating compounds in stinging nettle cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help dilate blood vessels around the eyes, improving blood flow and reducing swelling by reducing fluid buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, stinging nettle roots are used to reduce eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.
Barks: Containing bioactive flavonoids and triterpenoids, stinging nettle barks have been traditionally used to treat eye inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried urtica dioica leaves measuring one ounce and grind them into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Combine the powdered urtica dioica leaves with three tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for an additional hour to allow the mixture to infuse and cool slightly.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil for later use.
Mix two tablespoons of beeswax with one tablespoon of coconut oil and heat them in a double boiler until melted and smooth, then slowly add the infused urtica dioica oil and stir to combine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin, which can be particularly effective in reducing puffiness around the eyes. When applied topically, chamomile cream constricts blood vessels, reduces fluid buildup, and cools the skin, resulting in a reduction in eye swelling. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that may contribute to inflammation, leaving the skin looking smoother and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in chamomile cream neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye swelling, promoting healthy tissue repair and reducing inflammation.
Sothining and calming effects: Chamomile's soothing and calming properties help reduce eye irritation and discomfort by relaxing the muscles around the eyes and reducing stress-induced inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain apigenin, an flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye swelling.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers have soothing and calming effects on the eyes, making them a popular ingredient in eye creams for reducing swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them thoroughly in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 20% dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 80% coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted.
Allow the infused oil to cool and filter it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids.
Whip the filtered oil with 10% beeswax and 5% vitamin e oil using a hand mixer for 5 minutes to create a smooth consistency.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce puffiness and redness. The antioxidants present in lavender also soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, lavender's cooling effects help to constrict blood vessels, diminishing dark circles and crow's feet, leaving eyes looking refreshed and rejuvenated. By applying a gentle cream containing English lavender essential oil, individuals can experience a noticeable reduction in eye swelling and a more radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating discomfort and redness.
Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant properties help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from oxidative damage, reducing the appearance of puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Soothing and calming effects: The gentle, calming properties of lavender cream can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to eye swelling, allowing the eyes to relax and recover more effectively.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, which moisturize and calm the delicate skin around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning after dew has evaporated containing 2 cups of dried flowers per batch.
Combine 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut with 10g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and infuse for 12 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container removing excess solids after 4 hours.
Mix the strained oil with 20% beeswax by weight adding 30g of melted beeswax to the 100ml of infused oil while stirring gently until fully incorporated.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and store at room temperature for a shelf life of up to 6 months with a preparation time of 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut creams helps with eye swelling because they contain aescin, a powerful flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties. Aescin reduces fluid buildup in the eyes by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in inflammation, thereby decreasing swelling and puffiness. Additionally, horse chestnut extracts have been shown to improve blood circulation around the eyes, further reducing the appearance of dark circles and puffiness. By targeting both inflammation and circulation, herbal horse chestnut creams provide effective relief from eye swelling and related cosmetic concerns.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin is a saponin compound found in horse chestnut that helps to reduce inflammation and edema (swelling) by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory agents and improving blood vessel permeability, which alleviates puffiness and dark circles under the eyes.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in horse chestnut creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors, reducing the appearance of swelling and discoloration.

Saponins: Saponins, another type of compound found in horse chestnut, exhibit vasoconstrictive effects, causing blood vessels to constrict or narrow, which helps to reduce fluid accumulation and swelling around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing swelling and relieving discomfort associated with eye swelling.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and promote blood circulation, alleviating eye swelling symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50g of dried aesculus hippocastanum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered aesculus hippocastanum bark with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass bowl for 5 minutes.
Add 20 drops of chamomile essential oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the ingredients are fully incorporated and the oil has a warm, slightly thickened consistency.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or use immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with eye swelling because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and congestion. The cream's active ingredients, derived from the Maidenhair tree's leaves, have a soothing effect on the skin and eyes, gently calming puffiness and redness. As the cream is absorbed, it also cools and relaxes the surrounding tissues, further reducing swelling and discomfort. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for alleviating eye swelling, leaving eyes looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which may contribute to swelling.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels may improve blood flow to the eyes, reducing swelling and inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and pain in the eyes by inhibiting pro-inflammatory molecules.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye swelling.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them in the shade for 24 hours.
Grind 50g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20g of beeswax, 10g of coconut oil, and 5g of ginkgo bilboa powder in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 60°c before whipping until thickened for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with eye swelling because they are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The anthocyanins and polyphenols present in blueberries have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid retention. Additionally, the soothing properties of these creams can calm irritated eyes and provide long-lasting relief from eye swelling, making them an effective natural remedy for reducing puffy eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Blueberries are rich in antioxidants such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby reducing swelling.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal blueberry creams often contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid and quercetin, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with eye inflammation.
Moisturizing properties: The emollient properties of blueberries can help to soothe and hydrate the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation that may contribute to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the blueberry plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and puffiness around the eyes.
Seeds: The seeds of the blueberry plant contain antioxidants and fatty acids, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus leaves and dry them thoroughly in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Combine 100g of dried vaccinium myrtillus leaves with 500ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat for 3-4 hours or until it reaches a temperature of 70°c to 80°c.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The silymarin in milk thistle has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of calm around the delicate eye area. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties moisturize and soothe the skin, further reducing the appearance of swelling and leaving eyes looking refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: This flavonoid compound has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated or inflamed tissues around the eyes, reducing swelling.

Lectins: Some herbal milk thistle creams contain lectins, such as silybin, which have been shown to have a mild vasoconstrictive effect, helping to reduce blood flow and thereby alleviate congestion and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and antioxidants that promote skin health and reduce puffiness around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours or air drying.
Grind 20g of dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 5g of the powdered herb with 10g of beeswax and 15g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with eye swelling because of their unique composition. The mucilages in these creams create a soothing, protective barrier on the skin that reduces inflammation and irritation. This allows the eyes to calm down and return to their normal state, reducing the appearance of puffiness. Additionally, marshmallow creams' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the underlying causes of swelling, providing long-lasting relief for dry, tired, or irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can reduce inflammation and swelling around the eyes.
Moisturizing: The mucilages present in marshmallow root have excellent moisturizing properties, helping to soothe and hydrate dry, irritated eyes and reduce puffiness.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow root contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing swelling and redness.
Buds: Containing antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, they help to reduce puffiness and discomfort caused by swollen eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry althaea officinalis leaves and flowers to ensure optimal potency for subsequent use.
Combine 200g of dried althaea officinalis with 400ml of organic coconut oil in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to simmer for 30 minutes, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Add 10ml of beeswax and stir until fully incorporated before pouring the mixture into sterilized containers.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature over 2 hours before use or storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like citral and limonene, which have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in various parts of the body, including the eyes. When applied topically as a cream, lemon verbena can help to soothe and calm irritated or inflamed eyes, reducing puffiness and promoting a more refreshed and radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemon verbena, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye swelling and damage, promoting healthy tissue and reducing oxidative stress.

Vasodilatory properties: Lemon verbena's ability to dilate blood vessels (vasodilation) helps improve blood flow to the eyes, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling by removing excess fluid and promoting drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relieve itching around the eyes.
Buds: The buds contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly.
Steep 20g of dried verbena officinalis in 100ml of hot distilled water for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Add 30% beeswax to the liquid and heat it gently until fully incorporated and melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before transferring to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams help with eye swelling because they contain high levels of silica, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces puffiness and irritation. The cream's soothing properties calm the delicate skin around the eyes, while the antioxidant-rich formula neutralizes free radicals that can contribute to inflammation. As a result, herbal field horsetail creams provide fast and effective relief from eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other irritants, leaving your peepers feeling cool, calm, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes by reducing inflammation, making it an effective remedy for puffy or swollen eyes.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in horsetail cream help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to eye swelling and dark circles, promoting a smoother, more even appearance under the eyes.
Astringent: Horsetail's astringent properties help constrict blood vessels, reducing fluid retention and edema around the eyes, which can further exacerbate puffiness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce eye swelling by calming down inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Contain high levels of silica, which helps to strengthen blood vessels and reduce edema in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality equisetum arvense plant material of suitable size and quantity for preparation.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or desiccators within 24 hours of collection.
Grind the dried equisetum arvense into a fine powder weighing 200 grams to ensure uniform extraction.
Steep 50 grams of ground equisetum arvense in 500 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil for 2 weeks under room temperature conditions.
Strain and filter the infused oil mixture, then mix it with beeswax and sweet almond oil in a 1:1 ratio to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with eye swelling because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The active ingredients in fennel, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have a natural ability to calm and soothe the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing water retention and constriction of blood vessels. As a result, herbal fennel creams can help to alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or tiredness, leaving the eyes feeling refreshed, smooth, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel contains anti-inflammatory compounds like anethole, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant activity: Fennel's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to eye swelling, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting overall eye health.

Vasodilatory effects: Fennel's vasodilatory properties help dilate blood vessels in the eyes, which improves circulation and reduces swelling by allowing excess fluid and waste products to be removed more effectively.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing eye swelling and discomfort.
Buds: Fennel buds contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to soothe and calm inflamed eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and dry them thoroughly in shade for at least two hours before use.
Combine 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for seven days, shaking occasionally, then strain through cheesecloth and discard solids.
Mix the infused oil with 30 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut butter in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined.
Pour the warm cream into clean glass containers, allow to cool and set at room temperature for at least two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with eye swelling because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol present in peppermint oil reduces puffiness and soothes irritated eyes by constricting blood vessels and decreasing fluid accumulation. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation provides instant relief from itching and burning sensations associated with swollen eyes. As a result, applying herbal peppermint creams can effectively alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or dry eyes, promoting a clearer and more comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and swelling in the eyes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The menthol and methyl salicylate present in peppermint creams have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce puffiness and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Cicatrizant properties: Peppermint's cicatrizant compounds, such as flavonoids, can promote collagen synthesis and improve tissue repair, which may help reduce the appearance of dark circles and puffiness under the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Leaves: Fresh peppermint leaves contain menthol, which helps to reduce redness and itchiness associated with swollen eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Steam distill the peppermint leaves using a clevenger apparatus for at least three hours to extract the essential oil.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax with 40 grams of coconut oil and 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl, stirring until fully incorporated.
Add 2 milliliters of the peppermint essential oil to the melted wax mixture, stirring well to combine, and allow it to cool for five minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterile glass jars and allow it to solidify at room temperature over a period of one hour before using as an eye cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated eyes. The cream's active ingredients, such as eucalyptus and peppermint oils, work to reduce puffiness and redness by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation in the eyes. This natural remedy provides fast and gentle relief from eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus pressure, or other environmental factors, allowing for a more comfortable and clear visual experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eucalyptus globulus oil contains compounds like eucalyptol and globulol, which have anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce swelling in the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: The oil has antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to inflammation and swelling.
Soothing and calming effects: Eucalyptus globulus oil has a calming and soothing effect on the skin, which could potentially help reduce redness and discomfort associated with eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Roots: The roots of the plant have been traditionally used to treat various eye ailments, including swelling, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 20% eucalyptus globulus essential oil, 60% coconut oil, and 20% beeswax.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for approximately 10-15 minutes.
Add the coconut oil to the melted beeswax and stir until fully incorporated for about 5 minutes.
Combine the eucalyptus globulus essential oil with the melted coconut oil mixture and stir well for approximately 2 minutes.
Pour the final mixture into sterilized containers and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for eye swelling are topical treatments that utilize natural ingredients to reduce puffiness, inflammation, and discoloration around the eyes. These creams work by soothing the skin, reducing water retention, and improving circulation, which helps to alleviate the appearance of swollen eyes. Examples of herbal creams that help with eye swelling include those containing aloe vera, chamomile, and peppermint, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and calming properties. By using these creams, individuals can wake up looking refreshed and rejuvenated, feeling more confident and prepared to take on their day.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye swelling with herbal creams is by using natural ingredients that reduce inflammation and promote healing. Aloe vera gel, chamomile extract, and peppermint oil are effective ingredients in soothing and calming the affected area. Apply a thin layer of the cream or ointment to the swollen eye and gently massage it in until fully absorbed. Repeat as needed throughout the day for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye swelling that are treated with herbal creams are various skin conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil help soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing redness and itching. Other conditions like rosacea, acne, and minor burns can also be treated with herbal creams to promote healing and reduce discomfort.
